relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: The unveiling of Jesus Messiah comes as sign-given witness to servants.
John bears witness to what he saw.
Blessing rests on reading, hearing, and keeping the prophetic word.
Grace and peace flow from the Eternal One and the sevenfold Spirit.
Jesus is faithful witness, firstborn from death, ruler beyond kings.
He makes a priestly reign from those he loves and releases.
The cloud-coming one is seen by all, and earth mourns.
The Alpha and Omega speaks from eternal presence.
John is sibling and partner in pressure, reign, and endurance.
In Spirit, he hears the trumpet-voice.
The vision is sent to seven assemblies.
Turning toward the voice, he sees golden lampstands.
Among the lamps stands one like son of human.
White hair, fire-eyes, and shining presence reveal holy wisdom.
Bronze feet and many-water voice carry power.
Stars rest in his hand, word-sword from his mouth, sun-face shining.
Fear collapses, and his right hand says: Do not fear.
The Living One holds keys beyond death.
John is told to write what was seen, what is, and what comes.
Stars and lampstands are unveiled as messengers and assemblies.

contemplative_note: Revelation 1 is the opening of sight: the pressured sibling sees the Living One among the lampstands and hears the first command of apocalypse: do not fear.
